lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_normal_trace_existence_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation boundary portion partial Sigma_R, the normal trace of the restricted-continuation flux object exists as a certified normal-trace object whenever the explicitly listed flux-regularity, divergence-domain, and boundary-geometry hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_region_lipschitz_boundary
required_inputs.role: provides a normal-trace-admissible boundary chart structure for partial Sigma_R

required_inputs.name: flux_field_in_divergence_trace_domain
required_inputs.role: places the restricted-continuation flux field in the domain of the normal trace operator

required_inputs.name: divergence_regularitiy_on_restricted_region
required_inputs.role: supplies the divergence-side regularity required for a certified normal trace

required_inputs.name: normal_trace_operator_available_on_restricted_region
required_inputs.role: supplies the bounded normal trace map from the divergence-domain flux space to the boundary dual trace space

required_inputs.name: restriction_compatible_with_normal_boundary_chart
required_inputs.role: ensures the restricted-continuation cutoff/restriction does not destroy normal-trace admissibility

discharged_obligations: Separates normal trace existence from scalar boundary trace existence.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation normal trace exists.
Promotes normal trace existence from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove boundary flux pairing.
Does not prove integration by parts.
Does not identify the normal trace with a physical flux pairing.
Does not prove full RR closure.
